description: A Japanese dish based on vinegared rice combined with ingredients such as seafood, vegetables, egg, or seaweed. Sushi refers to the seasoned rice; it may be served as rolls, pressed pieces, or hand-formed portions.
preparation_steps: Cook and season the rice with vinegar mixture.
Shape the sushi by rolling, pressing, or hand-forming it.
Serve with condiments such as soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.

nutrition.protein: Low to high depending on fillings

health_advice.benefits: Can be relatively light and may provide lean protein and seafood-based nutrients.
health_advice.risks: Sodium can be high from soy sauce; raw fish requires strict freshness and food safety.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Balance rice-heavy rolls with simpler sushi and use sauces in moderation.

dietary_notes.note: Dietary suitability depends on fillings and condiments. Gluten-free versions require gluten-free soy sauce and careful ingredient selection.

allergens: Possible gluten in soy sauce or imitation seafood
